festivals.name: Great Wildebeest Migration (peak)
festivals.description: July's dramatic Mara River crossings are the world's greatest wildlife spectacle: 1.5 million wildebeest crossing crocodile-filled waters.

festivals.name: Lamu Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Kenya's oldest Swahili settlement hosts a festival of dhow racing, donkey races, poetry, and henna art.

destinations.name: Masai Mara
destinations.description: Kenya's crown jewel offers year-round big-five game drives and the world's greatest wildlife spectacle July–October.

destinations.name: Nairobi
destinations.description: The safari capital has a national park within city limits, the Karen Blixen Museum, and a thriving art and food scene.

destinations.name: Amboseli National Park
destinations.description: Africa's best destination for photography of big elephant herds with Mount Kilimanjaro as backdrop.

destinations.name: Tsavo National Parks
destinations.description: Kenya's largest park is home to red-dust-coated elephants and offers an off-the-beaten-path safari experience.

destinations.name: Lamu Island
destinations.description: East Africa's oldest living Swahili town is a UNESCO World Heritage site where donkeys are the only transport.

destinations.name: Lake Nakuru
destinations.description: A soda lake famous for vast flamingo flocks and one of Kenya's best places to see both white and black rhino.

destinations.name: Mount Kenya
destinations.description: Africa's second highest peak at 5,199m with dramatic glaciated peaks and diverse montane forest ecosystems.

destinations.name: Samburu National Reserve
destinations.description: A semi-arid reserve with unique species found only north of the equator: Grevy's zebra, reticulated giraffe, and Somali ostrich.
